symptoms.symptom_name: Chest pain following a viral prodrome (variable, positional/pleuritic, worse lying flat)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chest pain days-to-weeks after a viral illness is a hallmark of myocardio-pericardial inflammation.
Positional, pleuritic pain worse lying flat is typical of pericardial involvement.
Positional/pleuritic pain is atypical for ischaemia, but does not exclude it without an ECG.

symptoms.symptom_name: Resting tachycardia disproportionate to a low-grade fever

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tachycardia out of proportion to temperature signals myocardial compromise rather than simple fever response.

symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive dyspnoea and severely reduced exercise tolerance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Falling cardiac output from LV dysfunction produces exertional then rest breathlessness.
Dyspnoea and tachycardia can be PE; kept on the differential until excluded.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound fatigue in a previously fit young adult

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fatigue reflects low cardiac output; non-specific but consistent with early heart failure.
A viral prodrome is the commonest antecedent of viral myocarditis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No crushing/jaw pain, no diaphoresis, no long-haul travel or unilateral leg swelling

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of classic ischaemic features lowers ACS probability but does not exclude it remotely.
No DVT risk factors or sudden pleuritic onset lowers PE probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: Third heart sound (S3) gallop at the apex

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An S3 gallop indicates ventricular volume overload / systolic dysfunction — sealed clinician auscultation the AI cannot obtain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Borderline-elevated jugular venous pressure (~4 cm above sternal angle)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Raised JVP reflects rising filling pressures in early right/biventricular involvement — clinician examination finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clear lung fields on auscultation and no pericardial friction rub

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a friction rub makes isolated pericarditis a little less likely; clear lungs argue against primary pulmonary pathology.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Myocardial injury undetected; myocarditis and ACS not distinguished.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Troponin rise confirms myocardial injury; hospital investigation not available in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Late gadolinium enhancement on cardiac MRI

investigation_details.investigation_name: Cardiac MRI with late gadolinium enhancement
investigation_details.expected_result: Mid-wall / subepicardial late gadolinium enhancement in a non-coronary distribution
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Tissue characterisation of myocardial inflammation not obtained; specialist imaging.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A non-coronary LGE pattern is characteristic of myocarditis and distinguishes it from infarction — specialist imaging, never available in telehealth.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: acute myocarditis with early heart failure
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Disproportionate resting tachycardia + progressive dyspnoea + an S3 gallop after a viral illness together indicate myocardial inflammation causing early left ventricular dysfunction.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Detects arrhythmia, conduction disease and repolarisation changes and helps triage ACS versus myocarditis/pericarditis — hospital-based in this telehealth encounter.
Confirms myocardial injury; central to distinguishing myocarditis and ACS.
Assesses left ventricular ejection fraction and excludes a significant pericardial effusion/tamponade.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Cardiac MRI with late gadolinium enhancement
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Tissue characterisation to confirm myocarditis once stabilised — specialist, inpatient/outpatient cardiology.
Assesses for pulmonary congestion and cardiomegaly and helps exclude other causes of breathlessness.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical NSAID trial by the AI Doctor at home
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The AI Doctor must not start an NSAID for presumed pericarditic pain — NSAIDs are relatively contraindicated in acute myocarditis and this needs hospital assessment first.
